myAnswer: A method where the solution to a problem depends on solutions to smaller instances of the same problem (a function that calls itself, terminating at a base case).
derivation: Standard definition of recursion: self-referential decomposition into smaller instances until a base case.
shippedAnswer: A method where the solution to a problem depends on solutions to smaller instances of the same problem.

myAnswer: The OS capability of running more than one task/process apparently at the same time by time-slicing the CPU among them.
derivation: Multitasking = concurrent execution of multiple tasks sharing one CPU via rapid context switching.
shippedAnswer: Allowing a user to perform more than one computer task at the same time.

derivation: Read /figures/ce2016-q012.png: all eight cells (minterms 0-7) hold 1, so the whole map is covered; f = 1 (constant/tautology).
xy + xy' = x(y + y') = x.1 = x.
Round Robin is FCFS/FIFO plus a time quantum that preempts the running process; it is the preemptive form of FIFO, not of SJF (that is SRTF).
Balance is defined on subtree heights differing by at most 1, not on equal node counts; a balanced tree of 4 nodes can have 2 nodes left and 1 right. Statement is False.
The four recognised maintenance categories are corrective, adaptive, perfective, preventive; 'Customer Serviced' is not one of them.
A bridge is a layer-2 device that joins two LANs/LAN segments and forwards or filters frames by MAC address.
A repeater only regenerates the electrical/optical signal, so it lives in the physical layer.
Regenerates signal + connects LAN segments + no filtering = repeater; a bridge does filter, a router routes.

Oracle is the archetypal famous commercial DBMS and Linux is an OS, so C is the intended key; but MS Access (A) and MS SQL Server (B) are also commercial DBMS products, so the item literally has three true options. Answer C only under the 'most famous / most commercial' reading.
OSI reference model = 7 layers (physical, data link, network, transport, session, presentation, application).

Blind solve gave 7 (planning, requirements, design, implementation, testing, deployment, maintenance), the most widely published enumeration. The shipped key is 6 (which merges deployment into maintenance). The phase count is purely convention-dependent, so no option is objectively correct; I cannot break the tie on evidence, and I will not manufacture certainty. Follow the exam's own key (6) for revision.
Checking that the built results conform to the software specification is the Testing (verification) phase.
